violations.claim: PROVEN: filePath is always an absolute path — resolve() is total over absolute outputs
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 131
; PROVEN: filePath is always an absolute path — resolve() is total over absolute outputs
(declare-const args_has_positional Int)   ; 1 = args contains a non-flag element
(declare-const filePath_is_absolute Int)  ; 1 = filePath is an absolute path
; Code transition: branch 1 — no positional arg → || "." → resolve(".") → cwd (absolute)
(assert (=> (= args_has_positional 0) (= filePath_is_absolute 1)))
; Code transition: branch 2 — positional arg found → resolve(arg) → absolute (even if relative input)
(assert (=> (= args_has_positional 1) (= filePath_is_absolute 1)))
; Negation: filePath is not absolute
(assert (= filePath_is_absolute 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: "File: X" — X can be the current working directory when args has no positional arg
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 131
; VIOLATION: "File: X" — X can be the current working directory when args has no positional arg
(declare-const positional_arg_count Int)    ; number of args not starting with "-"
(declare-const filePath_is_directory Int)   ; 1 = filePath is a directory, not a TS file
; Code transition: args.find(a => !a.startsWith("-")) returns undefined when count = 0
(assert (= positional_arg_count 0))
; Code transition: undefined || "." fires → resolve(".") = cwd = a directory
(assert (=> (= positional_arg_count 0) (= filePath_is_directory 1)))
; Violation: log says "File:" but value is a directory
(assert (= filePath_is_directory 1))
(check-sat)
